Rankings City Performance — Tier 1
Rank Score Formula
Rank Score = pct(Utilization%) × 30 + pct(Acceptance%) × 20
+ pct(Combined Qty/Hr) × 30 + pct(Orders/Hr) × 20
Each metric is percentile-ranked (0–100) within the population, then weighted and combined. Score 50 = median. Score 80 = top 20%. Utilization % = % of logged-in time spent on ANY task (picking + stacking + bin audit + empty binning). Combined Qty/Hr = (items picked + items stacked) per hour — covers both picking and stacking output.
📌 Score 80 = this city's pickers are better than 80% of all G2 pickers across India

Picker Rankings Individual Performance
Performance Score (0–100) — What it means
Score = pct(Utilization%) × 30% + pct(Acceptance Rate%) × 20%
+ pct(Combined Qty/Hr) × 30% + pct(Orders/Hr) × 20%
Why percentile ranking? Instead of using raw numbers (which outliers can distort), each metric is first converted to a rank within the entire G2 population. Score = how a picker compares to ALL other G2 pickers today.

Utilization % = Busy time / Login time × 100. Busy time = time spent on picking + stacking + bin audit + empty binning combined. Not just picking.

Acceptance Rate % = Jobs accepted / Jobs assigned × 100. A picker declining jobs scores lower. Pickers with 0 assigned jobs are not penalized.

Combined Qty/Hr = (items picked + items stacked) / login hours. Covers both picking AND stacking throughput.

Orders/Hr = Orders completed / login hours. Picking-specific speed metric.
📌 Score 50 = exactly median · Score 80 = better than 80% of G2 fleet · Score 20 = bottom 20%

Action Required Flagged Pickers — Managers Take Note
🟡 Low Utilization — What it means
Utilization % < 50% (configurable)
This picker spent more than half their logged-in shift not working on any task. Utilization = Busy time / Login time × 100. A picker logged in for 8 hours but busy for only 3 hours has 37.5% utilization. Action: Find out if it's idle waiting (no jobs assigned), personal absence, or system issue.
🔴 High Decline Rate — What it means
Acceptance Rate % < 90% AND jobs were assigned
This picker is actively refusing jobs. Acceptance Rate = (Jobs assigned − Jobs declined) / Jobs assigned × 100. A picker assigned 100 jobs but declining 15 of them = 85% acceptance = flagged. Note: Pickers with 0 jobs assigned are excluded — they cannot decline what they never received. Action: HR conversation, investigate decline reasons.
⚫ Zero Productivity — What it means
Logged in (login time > 0) AND zero jobs assigned in ALL 4 task types
This picker clocked in but received zero job assignments across picking, stacking, bin audit, and empty binning simultaneously. Usually a system scheduling gap, not the picker's fault. Action: Check the job assignment system for that FC/date.
🔵 Slow Pick Time — What it means
Avg pick time > 2.5 min/SKU AND < 30 min (exclude data outliers)
This picker's average time to pick one SKU exceeds the 2.5 minute SLA. This directly contributes to breaking the 10-minute delivery promise. Example: a picker at 3.5 min/SKU picking a 10-item order adds 10 extra minutes before the rider even starts. Values above 30 min are statistical outliers (e.g. 1 order in a 5-hour shift) and are shown separately. Action: Speed coaching, check workstation layout.
Analytics Performance Distribution
📍 Utilization vs Acceptance Rate
each dot = one G2 picker
What this chart shows
X-axis (Utilization %): How much of each picker's logged-in time was spent on any task. Y-axis (Acceptance Rate %): What percentage of assigned jobs they accepted. Red dots: Pickers flagged on either metric (below threshold). Ideal picker: Top-right corner (high utilization + high acceptance). Hover over any dot to see the picker's name, FC, and exact values.
⏱ Where Does G2 Time Go?
busy time by task type
What this shows
G2 pickers don't just pick orders — they do 4 tasks. This chart shows the split of total active/busy minutes across: Picking (scanning and picking items), Stacking (stacking items in the FC), Bin Audit (checking bins), Empty Binning (handling empty bins). This is why utilization is calculated across ALL 4 tasks, not just picking.
Formula
Activity Mix % (Picking) = Picking_Time / (Picking_Time + Stacking_time + Bin_Audit_time + Empty_Binning_time) × 100
